title: Testing, Static Quality, Performance, and Release · Behavior Brief: Static Gates
summary: Harden the service-data client with behavioral tests, deterministic fixtures, performance evidence, and a clean release. Planned artifact: Add and verify the js static gates increment in a tested and release-ready JavaScript application. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Enforce formatting, linting, type-aware JavaScript checks, tests, dead-code detection, and dependency audit in local and CI gates.
Await asynchronous outcomes, control timers and network fixtures, and fail on unhandled work deterministically.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Static Gates
steps.instruction:
theory case — A release candidate passes the familiar 3-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Static Gates contract for the supplied data.

steps.title: Defend the Static Gates model

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Enforce formatting, linting, type-aware JavaScript checks, tests, dead-code detection, and dependency audit in local and CI gates.
